title: June in Hawke's Bay
meta_desc: Discover Hawke's Bay in June with kids, enjoy warm weather, Art Deco Napier, Cape Kidnappers gannets, and NZ's best fruit, a hidden family gem from $180-340/day for a family of 4
intro: Hawke's Bay is a wonderful destination for a family holiday in June. With mild winter weather, averaging 12-17°C, it's an ideal time to explore the region's many attractions. From the Art Deco city of Napier to the stunning natural beauty of Cape Kidnappers, there's something for everyone in Hawke's Bay.

sections.heading: Weather and What's On
sections.body:
June is a great time to visit Hawke's Bay, with calm and clear winter days. The region hosts several events in June, including the Hawke's Bay Winter Wine Weekend and the Napier Winter Deco Weekend.

These events offer a range of activities, from wine tastings to live music and dance performances. Be sure to check the local listings for dates and times.
list: Hawke's Bay Winter Wine Weekend: $50-$100 per person
Napier Winter Deco Weekend: free-$20 per person

sections.heading: Best Activities for June
sections.body:
In June, the Cape Kidnappers Gannet Colony is a must-visit, with over 20,000 gannets nesting on the cliffs. The Napier Art Deco walk is another highlight, with the city's stunning architecture on full display.

For some outdoor fun, head to Ocean Beach or Waimarama Beach for a day of swimming and sunbathing. If you're visiting with kids, the Napier Aquarium is a great place to spend an afternoon.
list: Cape Kidnappers Gannet Colony tour: $40-$60 per person

sections.heading: Booking Ahead and Crowd Levels
sections.body:
June is a relatively quiet month in Hawke's Bay, but it's still a good idea to book ahead for popular activities like the Cape Kidnappers Gannet Colony tour. The Napier Art Deco walk is self-guided, so you can explore at your own pace.

Crowd levels are generally low in June, making it a great time to visit if you prefer a more relaxed atmosphere.

sections.heading: Practical Tips for Families
sections.body:
When visiting Hawke's Bay with kids, be sure to pack layers for cooler winter mornings and afternoons. The region has plenty of family-friendly accommodation options, from motels to holiday parks.

For meals, there are many great cafes and restaurants in Napier and Hastings, with kid-friendly options like pizza and burgers.
list: Pack layers for cooler winter weather
Check out kid-friendly cafes and restaurants
Don't miss the free Napier Art Deco walking tour
Visit the Cape Kidnappers Gannet Colony at low tide for a more dramatic experience
